name: Public Key Infrastructure (PKI)
description: PKI binds identities to public keys through trusted certificate authorities: an entity generates a key pair and certificate signing request, the CA validates its identity and signs a certificate that chains to a trusted root; relying parties later verify the signature chain and revocation status before trusting it.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Needs certificate"]
    N2["Generate key pair"]
    N3["Create CSR"]
    N4["Submit to CA"]
    N5["CA validates identity"]
    N6{"Validation passes?"}
    N7["Reject request"]
    N8["CA signs certificate"]
    N9["Distribute + chain"]
    N10["Relying party verifies"]
    N11{"Valid + not revoked?"}
    N12["Trusted channel"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|no| N7
    N6 -->|yes| N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10
    N10 --> N11
    N11 -->|no| N7
    N11 -->|yes| N12

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N12 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Internet X.509 Public Key Infrastructure Certificate and CRL Profile (RFC 5280)
sources.authors: Cooper, D.; Santesson, S.; Farrell, S.; et al.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.

flowchartStandard.name: source_grounded_extraction_v2
flowchartStandard.sourceGrounding: Nodes, decisions, branches, and loops were extracted from the cited sources; each node carries a sourceRef to its supporting citation. Awaiting human spot-check before verified=true.
